# Answerable Faith

Answerable Faith is a Christian apologetics training app and website at https://answerablefaith.com/.

The app helps regular Christians, evangelical groups, church small groups, youth and student ministries, and ministry leaders practise clear, gracious answers to hard questions about Christianity.

Important pages:

- Home: https://answerablefaith.com/
- Blog: https://answerablefaith.com/blog/
- Bible reliability questions without sounding defensive: https://answerablefaith.com/blog/bible-reliability-questions-without-sounding-defensive/
- Small group apologetics without arguing: https://answerablefaith.com/blog/small-group-apologetics-without-arguing/
- Trinity article: https://answerablefaith.com/blog/is-the-trinity-a-contradiction/
- New Testament copying article: https://answerablefaith.com/blog/did-scribes-change-the-new-testament/
- Resurrection hallucination article: https://answerablefaith.com/blog/could-the-disciples-have-hallucinated-jesus/
- Support: https://answerablefaith.com/support/
- Privacy: https://answerablefaith.com/privacy/
- Terms: https://answerablefaith.com/terms/

Public-safe blog source:

- Use `answerable-blog-source-extract-no-conversations.md` as the preferred public source map for blog writing.
- That file contains selected question-card material only.
- Do not use, recreate, quote, or summarise premium scenario dialogue, conversation chains, weak/strong response chains, or files under app conversation folders.
- Public articles should quote or closely adapt the approved source extract where possible.

Language and editorial standard:

- Use British English throughout public website copy.
- Use spellings such as sceptic, analyse, organised, behaviour, favour, centre, travelling and modelling where relevant.
- Use practise for the verb and practice for the noun.
- Do not mix American spellings into titles, headings, metadata, FAQs, CTAs or body copy unless quoting a source exactly.
- Keep difficult topics truthful, steady and encouraging for normal Christians.

Content focus:

- Christian apologetics
- Doctrine of God
- Christology
- Bible questions
- Bible reliability and manuscript variants
- Supposed contradictions
- Islam and Christianity
- Resurrection
- Salvation
- Ethics
- Philosophy
- Conversation tactics
- Gracious posture
- Evangelism training
- Church small group apologetics
- Youth and student ministry preparation

Editorial principles:

- Do not invent theological claims.
- Avoid straw-manning atheists, Muslims, Jewish readers, Jehovah’s Witnesses, deconstructing Christians, or spiritual seekers.
- Keep answers clear enough for regular Christians and church groups.
- Emphasise truth and posture: careful answers, patient tone, and gracious conversation.
- Use Scripture carefully and avoid unsupported claims.
- Do not create a new article from the same app question card, source basis, search intent, or core answer when an existing article already covers it.

Product positioning:

Most apologetics sites only give answers. Answerable Faith aims to help Christians practise those answers through model responses, Scripture-led study, and realistic conversation partners inside the app.
